top_weaknesses.issue: Heavy MBA/VC-deck register: 'cleaner economic claim', 'operating model', 'defensible', 'sharper product discipline', 'messy middle' read as synthesis-engine prose, not lived founder voice
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace at least one abstraction-stack paragraph with a concrete operator anecdote (named workflow, named edge case, real $/time saved)

top_weaknesses.issue: List-shaped content (example tasks line 5; messy-middle components line 21) trapped in prose; mobile/SEO scannability hit
top_weaknesses.fix: Convert example tasks (invoices/support/claims/candidates/briefs/leads) and operating-system pieces (retrieval/permissions/audit logs/escalation/monitoring) to bullet lists

top_weaknesses.issue: Stat paragraph (61% VC / $155M agentic) reads like deck flex; HN crowd will demand source quality, and the linked domain looks low-trust
top_weaknesses.fix: Add primary-source citation language ('per PitchBook/CB Insights') or cut the number entirely; reframe so the stat supports the thesis instead of standing alone

top_weaknesses.issue: H2-only outline; chunky paragraphs without H3 break-up
top_weaknesses.fix: Add 1-2 H3s under longer sections, or add a one-line TL;DR after the lede

consensus_summary: The thesis lands and there are quotable lines ('cannot hide behind login metrics'; 'sells the job itself'), but the prose drifts into MBA register and the VC stats undermine credibility. Convert two list-shaped paragraphs to bullets and swap one abstraction block for a concrete operator example.
